title: snap
body:
Added snapcraft.yaml to build a [snap](https://snapcraft.io/) package for buildah. This Snap for buildah makes the installation and setup of buildah easier for the user by auto-updating to the latest version of buildah and can also rollback to the previous version if something breaks.

Signed-off-by: Michael Royal <mikerr90@outlook.com>
# Prep
sudo snap install snapcraft --classic
sudo snap install multipass --beta --classic

comments.body: @mikeroyal The CI thinks you didn't sign the PR.  Can you `git commit --amend -s` and the repush please?
I'm not snap literate, but the changes LGTM.  Is there anything that should/could be added to the install.md  page regarding snap usage, especially for the folks new to snap?

@mikeroyal Still have the first PR without a signing.

You can do a 

git rebase -i origin 

And then modify the first commit.
Finally do a git push --force

Hi @TomSweeneyRedHat, Here is install and setup info for snap usage.

# Install Snapd:

 $ sudo apt install snapd        # Ubuntu/Debian
 $ sudo dnf install snapd       # Fedora

To enable classic snap support for Fedora, enter the following to create a 
symbolic link between /var/lib/snapd/snap and /snap: 

 $ sudo ln -s /var/lib/snapd/snap /snap

# Install snap:

 $ sudo snap install buildah --edge --classic   # Ubuntu/Debian
 $ sudo snap install buildah --classic             # Fedora

Hi @rhatdan, 
Sorry about the delayed response the last few weeks at my job have been busy for me. So I've been thinking it might easier for you and others if the snap was in a separate repo for testing. So I created a separate repo that's all setup with a readme that you can clone here. https://github.com/mikeroyal/buildah-snap
This is an old PR that never got merged.  If people are still interested then ask me to reopen. Closing.
